description: Disulfiram-ethanol reaction (DER) acetaldehyde + ALDH-inhibition + dose anchors. The DER ceiling is what bounds the upper dose; the GSDMD threshold is what bounds the lower dose. Window = ratio between them.

DER_pathophysiology.mechanism: Disulfiram (via its metabolites — primarily Me-DTC) covalently inhibits hepatic ALDH2. Ethanol metabolism produces acetaldehyde via ADH; without functional ALDH, acetaldehyde accumulates 5-10× normal. Acetaldehyde >50 μM produces flushing, tachycardia; >100 μM produces hypotension + nausea + cardiovascular distress.
DER_pathophysiology.source: Yourick & Faiman 1989, PMID 2537080. Rat model with controlled ethanol challenge. Hypotension correlated with acetaldehyde, not ethanol; threshold 110 μM acetaldehyde requires ~40% ALDH inhibition.

clinical_DER_dose_response.source: Johansson 1989, PMID 2551696 — 100 mg DSF + ethanol challenge in volunteer subgroup
FDA Antabuse label + 70+ years clinical experience
FDA Antabuse label; cardiovascular collapse reported at very high ethanol exposures
Palatty 2011 PMC3056183 — 125 mg/day clinical use 60d with good tolerability; not challenged with ethanol
clinical_DER_dose_response.extrapolation_note: Below the 40% ALDH-inhibition threshold for hypotension (Faiman 1989). Plasma Me-DTC linearly scales with dose at this range (Johansson 1989), so 125 mg → ~70 nM Me-DTC peak << 278 nM at 400 mg.

clinical_DER_dose_response.source: Lee 2018 supp. text cites prior 62.5 mg/day disulfiram dosing in HIV PK studies

key_finding: DER threshold sits around DSF oral 100 mg + ethanol challenge. Below 100 mg/day, ALDH inhibition stays <40%, blood acetaldehyde stays <110 μM even with ethanol. This is the sub-AUD dose ceiling for the alcohol-deterrent effect.
biochemical_decoupling_caveat: Even at sub-DER doses, partial ALDH inhibition still occurs (15-40%). For patients with NO incidental ethanol exposure, this is irrelevant. For patients consuming kombucha, koji-fermented foods, certain medications (mouthwash with ethanol, IV preparations with ethanol vehicle), even mild ALDH inhibition can produce mild flushing. Practical implication: sub-AUD dose still requires abstinence from incidental ethanol but tolerates accidental low-level exposure better than 250-500 mg AUD dose.
